file_structure.description: Kubernetes Namespace for database operators and instances
Kubernetes Namespace for sigma1 services and shared resources
Deployment of Redis operator (e.g., from Spotahome or similar)
Terraform configuration for Cloudflare R2 bucket 'sigma1-assets' and credentials
Cloudflare Tunnel configuration for Morgan agent access

scaffolds.interfaces:
# Kubernetes Namespace for 'databases'
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: databases

# Kubernetes Namespace for 'sigma1'
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: sigma1

# CloudNative-PG Cluster (simplified example)
apiVersion: postgresql.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: sigma1-postgres
  namespace: databases
spec:
  instances: 1
  storage:
    size: 50Gi
  # ... other PostgreSQL configuration ...

# Redis/Valkey Cluster (conceptual, depends on chosen operator)
apiVersion: redis.redis.opstreelabs.in/v1beta1 # Example API version
kind: RedisCluster
metadata:
  name: sigma1-valkey
  namespace: databases
spec:
  clusterSize: 1
  kubernetesConfig:
    image: valkey/valkey:7.2-alpine
  # ... other Redis/Valkey configuration ...

# Cloudflare Tunnel (simplified example)
apiVersion: networking.cfargotunnel.com/v1alpha1
kind: Tunnel
metadata:
  name: sigma1-morgan-tunnel
  namespace: sigma1
spec:
  # ... tunnel configuration ...

# ConfigMap for Infrastructure Endpoints
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: sigma1-infra-endpoints
  namespace: sigma1
data:
  POSTGRES_URL: "postgresql://sigma1_user:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@sigma1-postgres.databases.svc.cluster.local:5432/sigma1"
  REDIS_URL: "redis://sigma1-valkey.databases.svc.cluster.local:6379"
  S3_BUCKET_NAME: "sigma1-assets"
  S3_ENDPOINT: "https://<cloudflare-r2-account-id>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com"
  S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID: "$(S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID)"
  S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: "$(S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY)"
scaffolds.pattern_examples: The `accesstunnels.networking.cfargotunnel.com` CRD is available, indicating that Cloudflare Tunnel resources can be defined using Kubernetes Custom Resources. Follow this pattern for `Tunnel` definitions.
scaffolds.test_stubs:
# Verification Checklist for Core Infrastructure Setup

## Namespace Verification
- [ ] `kubectl get ns databases`
- [ ] `kubectl get ns sigma1`

## Database Operator & Instance Verification
- [ ] `kubectl get pods -n databases -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloudnative-pg` (Verify CNPG operator is running)
- [ ] `kubectl get cluster -n databases sigma1-postgres` (Verify PostgreSQL cluster exists)
- [ ] `kubectl get pods -n databases -l app.kubernetes.io/name=sigma1-postgres` (Verify PostgreSQL pods are running)
- [ ] `kubectl get pods -n databases -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redis-operator` (Verify Redis operator is running)
- [ ] `kubectl get rediscluster -n databases sigma1-valkey` (Verify Valkey cluster exists)
- [ ] `kubectl get pods -n databases -l app.kubernetes.io/name=sigma1-valkey` (Verify Valkey pods are running)

## Connectivity & Functionality Tests
- [ ] Connect to PostgreSQL from a temporary pod in `sigma1` namespace:
  ```bash
  kubectl run -it --rm --image=postgres:latest --namespace sigma1 -- psql -h sigma1-postgres.databases.svc.cluster.local -U sigma1_user -d sigma1
  # Inside psql: \l, \dt, SELECT 1;
  ```
- [ ] Connect to Redis/Valkey from a temporary pod in `sigma1` namespace:
  ```bash
  kubectl run -it --rm --image=valkey/valkey:7.2-alpine --namespace sigma1 -- valkey-cli -h sigma1-valkey.databases.svc.cluster.local
  # Inside valkey-cli: PING, SET mykey myvalue, GET mykey
  ```

## S3/R2 Verification
- [ ] Confirm 'sigma1-assets' R2 bucket exists via Cloudflare dashboard or `rclone ls r2:sigma1-assets`
- [ ] Verify S3 credentials allow read/write access (e.g., using `aws s3 cp` or `rclone copy`)

## ConfigMap Verification
- [ ] `kubectl get configmap sigma1-infra-endpoints -n sigma1 -o yaml`
- [ ] Verify `data` fields contain correct connection strings and placeholder credentials.

## Cloudflare Tunnel Verification
- [ ] `cloudflared tunnel status sigma1-morgan-tunnel` (Requires `cloudflared` CLI configured)
- [ ] Verify tunnel is active and connected.
- [ ] Attempt to reach a placeholder service via the tunnel's public hostname.
